verse_text: And suddenly there came a sound from heaven, as of a rushing mighty wind, and it filled the whole house where they were sitting.

links.context: Fire in this context has the primary meaning of the gift of the Holy Spirit, which is given to the world at Pentecost ([[ACT.2:1]]-4). It further declares the judgment of Christ, in which the faithless will burn (see [[2TH.1:6]]-10; 2:8). Note that this fire is one. It is the same Power and the same Spirit which both enlivens the faithful and destroys the faithless.

symbolic.title: Returning to the Garden of Eden

links.context: New tongues refers to the ability to speak in languages that one has not learned in order to edify others in worship (1Co 14) and to preach the gospel ([[ACT.2:1]]-11).

symbolic.title: Returning to the Garden of Eden

links.context: The wind being called upon to blow upon the garden can be likened to the Holy Spirit, the strong wind that blew on the Apostles beginning in the upper room ([[ACT.2:2]]).

symbolic.title: Returning to the Garden of Eden
